summary: DHH reports that Apple has finally updated iSync to version 2.1, adding support for his Nokia 6630 phone. He had purchased the phone months earlier expecting Apple's Tiger OS to support it, and was disappointed when it didn't. The brief post celebrates the belated arrival of interoperability between his phone and Mac's Address Book and iSync.
key_points: Apple's iSync 2.1 update finally added Nokia 6630 support months after Tiger launched
DHH had bought the Nokia 6630 in December expecting Tiger would deliver interoperability
The update came unexpectedly ('out of the blue') and resolved a longstanding frustration
money_quotes: I bought the fantastic Nokia 6630 back in December under the assumption that Tiger would deliver on interoperability. How sadly disappointed I was when it didn't.
But now, finally and out of the blue, Apple has updated iSync to version 2.1 and alongside Address Book now supports my lovely phone. Happy joy, joy.
key_insight: A brief personal note celebrating Apple finally delivering phone-Mac interoperability that had been expected for months.
